summary: Electrifying Janis Lovelight jam and fierce Not Fade Away → St Stephen
blurb: The show delivers a scorching Turn On Your Lovelight featuring Janice Joplin and Pigpen, plus a high‑energy second set highlighted by Not Fade Away into Saint Stephen. Jerry’s guitar shines throughout, while Bob Weir locks in on the jams. A must‑listen for anyone chasing classic 1970 Dead fire.
review:
July 16 1970 at San Rafael’s Euphoria Ballroom stands out as one of those rare nights where a guest appearance and the band’s own chemistry collide into pure magic. The first set builds to an unforgettable Turn On Your Lovelight, where Janis Joplin’s raw howl meets Ron “Pigpen” McKernan’s growling mic‑solo and Jerry Garcia’s sexy, perfectly synced guitar lines—an electrifying moment that still feels fresh on the tape. Candyman follows with soaring vocal harmonies, while the China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue glides seamlessly into the set’s climax.

The second set is a tour‑de‑force of Dead intensity: a blistering Not Fade Away jam erupts into a flawless Saint Stephen transition, underscored by Jerry’s razor‑sharp tone and Bob Weir “on top of his game.” Attics Of My Life provides a gorgeous, lyrical contrast, and the band’s tight interplay on Cumberland Blues keeps the momentum rolling. With both soundboard recordings capturing crystal‑clear detail and an audience tape that preserves the raw live energy despite some hiss, this night remains a benchmark for 1970 performances.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are described with positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the Lovelight jam, Not Fade Away > Saint Stephen transition, and overall high energy. No poor‑song or performance criticisms appear, indicating strong consensus of excellence across multiple sources.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape captures the unique Janis & Pigpen Lovelight moment with clear enough detail, making it the most complete representation of this iconic guest performance.

key_highlights: Janis Joplin & Pigpen jam on Turn On Your Lovelight
Fierce Not Fade Away → Saint Stephen transition
Beautiful Attics Of My Life rendition
High‑energy second set with tight band interplay

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers sexy, perfectly synced guitar work on the Lovelight jam and showcases razor‑sharp tone during Not Fade Away and the seamless NFA → St Stephen transition.
band_performance.Bob: Weir is on top of his game, offering standout rhythm/lead interplay on Not Fade Away and solid support throughout the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides a solid rhythmic foundation that drives the high‑energy jams without slipping.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in with Mickey, keeping the groove tight during both the first‑set jam and the second‑set fireworks.
